prompt: Create a square album cover for a melancholic indie/alternative single. Canvas: 1:1 ratio, softly textured paper background fading from warm cream on the left to pale powder blue on the right, with subtle film grain and a vintage editorial finish. Composition: place a blurred, painterly portrait of a person on the right two-thirds of the cover, cropped from mid-torso upward, head tilted back and turned slightly upward, with short dark messy hair forming a soft silhouette. The face is intentionally obscured by a rectangular frosted blur block in the upper-right area, semi-opaque with muted beige, gray, and brown tones. Clothing: a richly textured mustard-gold faux-fur coat or shawl sweeping dramatically from the lower-left corner across the body toward the right, with motion blur and warm amber shadows; underneath, a dark patterned high-neck garment in burgundy, green, and charcoal. Typography: on the left side, set exactly 2 text elements. Text element 1 is the title Encore Une Fois, stacked across three lines, very large, elegant high-contrast serif typeface, thin strokes, dark charcoal gray, aligned left, occupying the upper-left quadrant. Text element 2 is the artist name PZF, small uppercase serif letters with wide tracking, placed below the title near the left-center. Visual style: romantic, cinematic, slightly surreal, soft-focus photographic painting, warm ochre and burnt orange contrasted with cool blue, nostalgic French art-pop mood. Constraints: keep the design readable at thumbnail size, preserve generous negative space around the typography, no extra logos, no barcode, no additional text.
